title_en: Parents Fell in Love With Alpha School’s Promise. Then They Wanted Out
title_zh: 父母曾迷上Alpha School的“承诺”，但后来却想要退学
rubric_en: In Brownsville, Texas, some families found a buzzy new school’s methods—surveillance of kids, software in lieu of teachers—to be an education in and of itself.
rubric_zh: 在得克萨斯州布朗斯维尔，一些家庭发现这所热闹的新学校所采用的做法——对孩子的监控、用软件取代老师——本身就像一次特殊的“教育”。

paragraphs.en: One day last fall, Kristine Barrios’ 9-year-old daughter got stuck on a lesson in IXL, the personalized learning software that served as her math teacher. She had to multiply three three-digit numbers without using a calculator. Then she had to do it again, her mom says, more than 20 times, without making mistakes.
paragraphs.zh: 去年秋天的一天，克里斯蒂娜·巴里奥斯9岁的女儿在IXL这款个性化学习软件课上卡住了。该软件在她的数学课上相当于老师。她需要不用计算器计算三个三位数相乘。然后，正如她妈妈所说，她不得不在没有算错的情况下，把这道题重复做了二十多次。

paragraphs.en: At Alpha School, the private microschool the girl and her younger brother attended in Brownsville, Texas, she had been working a grade level ahead of her age in math, Barrios says. She could do three-digit multiplication correctly most of the time. But whenever she made an error in IXL, the software would determine she needed more practice and assign her more questions. She told her mom that she had asked her “guide,” the adult who supervised her classroom in lieu of a teacher, to make an exception and let her move on. She said the guide’s reply was that she needed to get it done, that it was expected of her.
paragraphs.zh: 在艾尔法学校（Alpha School）——这所位于得州布朗斯维尔的私营小型校，巴里奥斯的女儿和弟弟就读——她的数学水平本应比同龄人快一整年。她大多数时候都能正确做出三位数乘法。但每当她在IXL中出错，软件就会判断她需要更多练习，并给她布置更多题目。她告诉妈妈说，她曾请求她的“学习指导员”——那个取代老师在课堂中监督学生的成年人——破例让她继续下一步。对方回答说，她必须把题做完，因为这是对她的要求。

paragraphs.en: The adult guides in Alpha’s classrooms “don't do any teaching,” says the current head of the Brownsville school.
paragraphs.zh: Alpha学校的成人“指导员”“并不负责真正教学”，布朗斯维尔校区的现任负责人如是说。

paragraphs.en: Who is Ed Zitron? What is Ed Zitron? How is Ed Zitron? Tommy Craggs profiles the man, the myth, the hater.
paragraphs.zh: 谁是Ed Zitron？Ed Zitron是什么样的人？Ed Zitron最近如何？汤米·克拉格斯（Tommy Craggs）正在对这个人、这个神话、这个反对者进行剖析。

paragraphs.en: Over the next weekend, Barrios says, she and her husband sat with their daughter for hours each day until she finished the multiplication lesson, even as she broke down and sobbed that she’d rather die than keep going. Ultimately, Barrios says she double-checked all the answers on a calculator before the 9-year-old entered them. But when the girl returned to school with the lesson completed, her mom says, she came back reporting crushing news: In the time she had spent stuck, she had fallen even farther behind her targeted goals.
paragraphs.zh: 在接下来的周末，巴里奥斯说，她和丈夫每天都和女儿在一起坐了好几个小时，直到她把乘法课做完。尽管女儿崩溃大哭地说，她宁愿死也不想继续，巴里奥斯仍然坚持。最终，她说自己在孩子写答案前，用计算器把所有答案都核对了一遍。但当女孩带着完成的作业回到学校时，她告诉妈妈一个令人沮丧的消息：在她被困着学习的这段时间里，她离自己的目标反而更远了。

paragraphs.en: Within a couple weeks, Barrios says, the school reported to her and her husband that their daughter wasn’t eating lunches. According to Barrios, Alpha said it was “because she would rather stay in and work.” The girl later explained to her parents that she was spending lunchtimes catching up on IXL. (In a statement to WIRED, IXL representatives wrote that Alpha School’s account was deactivated this past July and claims that it is “no longer an IXL customer due to violating our terms of service,” adding that IXL “is not intended—and we do not recommend its use—as a replacement” for “trained, caring teachers.”)
paragraphs.zh: 大约两周后，巴里奥斯说，学校告诉她和丈夫，他们的女儿在不吃午餐。根据巴里奥斯的说法，Alpha 解释说这是因为她“宁愿留下来继续学习”。后来女孩向父母解释说，她中午用来补习 IXL（是一种自适应学习平台）。（据WIRED的声明，IXL 的代表称，Alpha School 的账户在今年7月被停用，并表示由于其违背了服务条款，IXL “不再是其客户”；他们补充道，IXL “并非—也不建议作为—经过训练且有爱心的教师的替代品。”）

paragraphs.en: When Barrios’ husband brought their daughter to a previously scheduled checkup soon after, her doctor noted with concern that she had lost a significant amount of weight in a short time. Her dad then brought her to school with a note from the pediatrician, Barrios says, instructing her to eat snacks in between regular meals and saw her walk into school with it in her hand. She told her parents she delivered it to staff. Even though Alpha had asked parents in its handbook to “refrain” from sending in “midday snacks,” Barrios and her husband wanted to follow the pediatrician's recommendation, she says.
paragraphs.zh: 不久之后，巴里奥斯的丈夫按原定时间带女儿去做了一次体检。医生担忧地指出，她在短时间内体重下降明显。随后父亲拿着儿科医生的建议条带她去学校，巴里奥斯说，医生建议她在两餐之间吃点零食；她亲眼看到女儿拿着纸条走进学校。女孩告诉父母，她已把纸条交给了工作人员。尽管 Alpha 在手册里要求家长“不要”递送“午间零食”，但巴里奥斯和丈夫还是想按儿科医生的建议执行。

paragraphs.en: For the first few days, Barrios says, her daughter ate her snacks. Then one afternoon she returned with them still in her backpack, uneaten. Barrios, alarmed, asked if Alpha was providing different food instead. No, the 9-year-old answered. She told her mom that staff at the school said she didn’t earn her snacks and wouldn’t get them until she met her learning metrics.
paragraphs.zh: 在最初几天里，巴里奥斯说，女孩会把零食吃掉。后来一个下午，她带着还没动过的零食回家，依旧装在书包里。巴里奥斯心里一惊，便问学校是否给她提供了别的食物。9岁的她说不是。她告诉妈妈，学校的工作人员说她还没“赚到”零食，只有达到学习指标才会发。

paragraphs.en: “ As a parent, you're like, this is not OK,” Barrios recalls. She pulled both her kids out of Alpha School that November.
paragraphs.zh: “作为家长，你会想到：‘这不行。’” 巴里奥斯回忆道。她在那年11月把两个孩子都从阿尔法学校撤了出来。

paragraphs.en: Barrios and her husband joined a growing group of families who had chosen to leave the school’s Brownsville campus. About two dozen kids were in the inaugural class in 2022, according to Paige Fults, the current head of Alpha School Brownsville. At least five families (including some with more than one child) have departed. That hasn’t stopped Alpha’s leaders from pointing to Brownsville as an example, in at least one white paper and in applications to open new charter schools, of how the proprietary model Alpha uses, 2 Hour Learning, can succeed in communities with “low SES" (meaning socioeconomic status).
paragraphs.zh: 巴里奥斯和她的丈夫加入了一个不断扩大的家庭群体，这些家庭选择离开学校在布朗斯维尔的校区。现任阿尔法学校布朗斯维尔分校负责人佩吉·弗尔茨（Paige Fults）称，2022年首届班级共有约二十名学生。至少有五个家庭（其中一些家庭有不止一个孩子）已经离校。这并没有阻止阿尔法的领导层把布朗斯维尔拿来当样板——在至少一份白皮书和若干申请开设新特许学校的材料中，他们都把阿尔法采用的专属模式“2 Hour Learning”称为在“低社会经济地位（low SES）”社区也能成功的案例。

paragraphs.en: With 2 Hour Learning, which is in use not only at Alpha but also a host of its private “sister schools,” students are meant to spend just two hours per day on “learning sessions.” Personalized learning software—or what the 2 Hour Learning homepage began referring to last year as an “AI tutor”—does the teaching. MacKenzie Price, one of the founders of Alpha and 2 Hour Learning, told WIRED: “ Our students are learning twice as much, our classes are top 1 percent across grades and subjects, and we're doing it all in a much, much shorter amount of time.” (Price’s claim is based in part on comparisons of standardized test data. While Price initially said that Alpha would share its data with WIRED, it has not done so.)
paragraphs.zh: 在不仅阿尔法学校，而是其众多私立“姊妹校”也在使用的“2 Hour Learning”模式中，学生每天只需花两小时在“学习课程”上。个性化学习软件——也就是“2小时学习”官网去年开始称作“AI导师”——负责教学。阿尔法与2 Hour Learning的联合创始人之一、麦肯齐·普莱斯（MacKenzie Price）对《WIRED》说：“我们的学生学得更多，是两倍；我们的班级在各年级各科中都排在前1%；而且我们是在更短得多的时间内完成的。”（普莱斯的说法部分基于标准化考试数据的比较。虽然她最初表示阿尔法愿意与《WIRED》分享数据，但最终并未实现。）

paragraphs.en: As the nation confronts a teacher shortage crisis, Alpha’s audacious promise has propelled it from a small Texas private school into a budding educational empire. It’s a darling of the Trump administration and the very wealthy. Joe Liemandt, a Stanford dropout turned tech founder whose billions come from selling automation software, is the school’s “principal.” This past summer, hedge fund manager Bill Ackman promoted the school on X and hosted a panel about it in the Hamptons. Reid Hoffman, the cofounder of LinkedIn, has had Price on his podcast and has said on X that the “best news” is that Alpha’s AI tutor approach “can be a reality for every student, anywhere.” In September, US secretary of education Linda McMahon visited the original Austin campus and said the models presented there were “the most exciting thing I’ve seen in education in a long time.”
paragraphs.zh: 在全国面临教师短缺危机的背景下，阿尔法大胆的承诺推动这所原本只是德州一家小型私校的机构，成长为萌芽中的教育帝国。它深受特朗普政府和极其富有阶层的青睐。乔·利曼德（Joe Liemandt）——这位从斯坦福辍学、后来成为科技创始人、凭借自动化软件销售致富的企业家——是该校的“校长”。今年夏天，对冲基金经理比尔·阿克曼（Bill Ackman）在X平台上推荐该校，并在汉普顿举办了关于它的专题讨论会。LinkedIn联合创始人里德·霍夫曼（Reid Hoffman）邀请普莱斯上了他的播客，并在X上表示，“最振奋人心的消息”是阿尔法的AI导师模式“在任何地方都能让每个学生成为现实”。9月，美国教育部长琳达·麦克马洪（Linda McMahon）访问了最初的奥斯汀校园，并表示那里的模型“是我最近在教育领域看到过的最令人兴奋的东西”。

paragraphs.en: The school is in the midst of a national expansion, including roughly a dozen new campuses in Arizona, California, Florida, New York, North Carolina, and Virginia to add to the five already open in Texas. An “affiliate” charter school called Unbound Academy is enrolling students in Arizona. Although Unbound is independent from Alpha, its leadership team includes Price and her husband, Andrew, and some of the other initial members of its board either work at or have connections to Alpha. Unbound’s application to open in Arizona cited Alpha School Brownsville, saying that it “demonstrated how the 2hr Learning Model can effectively address educational disparities” and make “high-quality education accessible to all students.”
paragraphs.zh: 该校正处于全国扩张之中，计划在亚利桑那州、加利福尼亚州、佛罗里达州、纽约州、北卡罗来纳州和弗吉尼亚州新增大约十二个新校区，补充德克萨斯州已开设的五个校区。一所名为 Unbound Academy 的“附属”特许学校正在亚利桑那州招生。尽管 Unbound 在组织上独立于 Alpha，但其领导团队包括 Price 及其丈夫 Andrew，且其最初董事会成员中的一些人要么在 Alpha 工作，要么与 Alpha 有关联。Unbound 在申请在亚利桑那州开校时，引用了 Alpha School Brownsville，称该校“展示了 2 小时学习模式能够有效解决教育差距”，并让“优质教育对所有学生都可获得”。

paragraphs.en: Certainly, the school has its fans: When Newsweek recently visited the Brownsville campus, one older student said she appreciated her time there so much that she was looking to found an upper school herself so that she and her cohort could continue with an Alpha-like model rather than attend the local public high school. “The Rio Grande Valley, Brownsville, and all the cities surrounding it are lagging behind in education in the US, so students going from a fast-paced environment like Alpha and then having to cut it off and go to a traditional school doesn't make sense," the 12-year-old said. (After offering in August to connect WIRED with this student, Alpha did not respond to subsequent requests to do so.)
paragraphs.zh: 当然，这所学校也有拥趸：WIRED 最近访问布朗斯维尔校区时，一位年长学生表示，她非常喜欢在那里上学，正计划自己创办一所高年级学校，好让她和同届同学能够继续沿用类似 Alpha 的模式，而不是去上当地公立高中。她说：“里奥格兰德河谷、布朗斯维尔以及周边各城都在美国教育上落后，所以学生从 Alpha 这种快节奏环境毕业后，突然被迫转去传统学校上学，这是说不通的。”（该学生在8月曾应邀让 WIRED 与她联系，但 Alpha 后来未回应后续的采访请求。）

paragraphs.en: But in interviews with WIRED, more than a dozen former employees, students, and parents say what they expected from Alpha School wasn’t what it delivered. Former “guides” from different campuses, many of whom requested anonymity because they fear negative consequences, say Alpha’s educational philosophy was driven by software metrics and, sometimes, Liemandt's whims. One guide said they believed Alpha wanted to prepare students for a hypercompetitive “late capitalism, dog-eat-dog” environment. Parents like Kristine Barrios say the school impacted their children, left them with glaring gaps in their education, and is now using them to sell a story of success. “They set her up for failure,” Barrios says, and then it felt like “they punished her for failing.”
paragraphs.zh: 但在与 WIRED 的采访中，十多名前雇员、学生和家长表示，Alpha School 并未兑现他们原本对它的期待。来自不同校区的前“指导员”中，许多人要求匿名，因为他们担心会受到不利影响，称 Alpha 的教育理念由软件指标驱动，并有时受 Liemandt 的个人喜好所左右。一位指导员说，他们认为 Alpha 想把学生培养成适应高度竞争、后资本主义式“弱肉强食”环境的人。像 Kristine Barrios 这样的家长表示，这所学校影响了他们的孩子，留下了明显的学习空白，而现在又拿孩子的经历来包装“成功故事”。“他们把孩子推向失败，”Barrios 说，“然后又像是在惩罚她因为失败而付出的代价。”

paragraphs.en: In response to WIRED’s October 10 requests for comment for this story, Alpha School shared a Google Doc of partial responses six days later that it subsequently rescinded access to. On October 20, Alpha informed WIRED: “We possess records that materially contradict key elements of your current reporting.” After several extensions of the original deadline requested for response, WIRED received no further substantive replies from Alpha to the requests sent on October 10. Last Friday morning, October 24, WIRED received a letter from lawyers for Alpha seeking more time to respond and further information from WIRED, including “waivers” from individual students’ parents. That night, Alpha’s lawyers sent WIRED a statement reading, in part: “Allegations that Alpha has mistreated, punished, or caused harm to any student are categorically and demonstrably false. Alpha and its employees prioritize a safe and productive environment to accelerate academic mastery and allow students to thrive.”
paragraphs.zh: 10 月 10 日，WIRED 就本报道向 Alpha School 发出评论请求后，学校在六天后分享了一份包含部分回应的 Google 文档，随后又撤回了访问权限。10 月 20 日，Alpha 通知 WIRED：我们掌握的资料“在实质上与您当前报道中的关键要点相矛盾”。在原定回应截止日期被多次延长后，WIRED 在10月10日发出的后续提问中未再收到 Alpha 的实质性回复。上周五早晨——10月24日，WIRED 收到 Alpha 律师发来的信件，要求延长回复时间并从 WIRED 索取更多信息，包括“各学生家长的豁免声明”。当晚，Alpha 律师向 WIRED 发出一份声明，其中部分内容为： “指控 Alpha 有虐待、惩罚或对任何学生造成伤害的说法，在本质上和事实层面都是错误的。Alpha 和其员工始终将提供安全且高效的学习环境作为优先，以加速学术掌握并让学生茁壮成长。”

paragraphs.en: When scientists want to condition lab animals to perform tasks repetitively, they may use a Skinner box, a device invented by Harvard psychologist B. F. Skinner that rewards test subjects for responding to certain stimuli. In 1953, Skinner adapted the idea into one of the world’s first personalized learning machines. On parents’ day in his daughter’s fourth-grade class, he noticed that some students were bored waiting for the teacher to review their math work while others were struggling to keep up. Skinner got to tinkering and ultimately developed boxes that could accept questions on punch cards and two-digit answers via levers. When a student got the question right, the box would shine a light to let them know they could move on.
paragraphs.zh: 当科学家希望训练实验动物反复执行任务时，他们可能会使用一种称为斯金纳箱的装置。它是哈佛心理学家B·F·斯金纳发明的，通过对实验对象对某些刺激做出反应进行奖励。1953年，斯金纳将这一理念改造成了世界上最早的个性化学习机器之一。在他女儿四年级的家长见面会上，他注意到有些学生在等老师讲评数学作业时显得无聊，而另一些则在跟不上进度。斯金纳于是开始琢磨并最终开发出一种装置：它可以通过凸轮卡片输入问题，也能通过杠杆输入两位数答案。学生答对题目后，机器会亮起灯光，告诉他们可以继续下一题。

paragraphs.en: After the digital revolution, learning machines got more adaptive. Personalized software could present students with questions that changed in difficulty and subject matter based on previous answers. By 2014, a small group of wealthy families from Austin’s burgeoning tech scene—including entrepreneur Brian Holtz and MacKenzie Price—felt confident enough in the software and in their own backgrounds to bet their kids’ educations on a new model. They founded Emergent Academy, renamed Alpha School in 2019. Some of the earliest students included Liemandt’s kids. The core philosophy, says Graham Frey, the school’s CEO from 2017 until 2022, was “the only way we’re going to know if the apps work is if we let them do the teaching.”
paragraphs.zh: 随着数字革命到来，学习机器变得更加自适应。个性化软件可以根据学生先前的答案，动态调整题目的难度和学科内容。到2014年，包括企业家布莱恩·霍尔茨（Brian Holtz）和麦肯齐·普赖斯（MacKenzie Price）在内的一小群来自奥斯汀蓬勃发展的科技圈的富裕家庭，已经对这类软件和自己的判断能力足够有信心，开始把孩子的教育押注在一种全新的模式上。他们创办了“Emergent Academy”，并于2019年改名为阿尔法学校。该校早期学生中就包括利曼德特（Liemandt）家的孩子。该校前首席执行官格雷厄姆·弗雷（Graham Frey）表示，核心理念是“我们唯一能知道这些应用是否有效的方法，就是让它们来教学”。

paragraphs.en: Do the machines have blood on their hands? Will Knight reports from the front.
paragraphs.zh: 这些机器是否“沾满了鲜血”？威尔·奈特从前线为读者带来最新调查。

paragraphs.en: As Alpha was getting off the ground in Austin, the tech world’s billionaire philanthropists were coming around to a similar vision of the future of education. In Mark Zuckerberg and Priscilla Chan’s 2015 “Letter to Max,” released with the launch of their nonprofit, they pledged to build a world of new opportunity for their firstborn child and her peers. “Our generation grew up in classrooms where we all learned the same things at the same pace regardless of our interests or needs,” they wrote. “You’ll have technology that understands how you learn best” and get “as much help as you need in your most challenging areas.”
paragraphs.zh: 就在阿尔法学校在奥斯汀起步之际，科技界的亿万富豪慈善家们也逐步接受了类似的教育未来愿景。马克·扎克伯格和普莉斯拉·陈（Priscilla Chan）在2015年随其非营利机构发布的《写给麦克斯的一封信》里承诺，为他们的长子和同龄人打造一个机会更均等的世界。信中写道："我们这一代人在教室里成长，那里每个人都以同样的速度、学习同样的内容，而不考虑个人兴趣和需求"。他们称，“你将拥有能够理解你如何学习得最好的技术”，并在“最具挑战的领域"得到“足够的帮助”。

paragraphs.en: Zuckerberg’s initiative committed to spending more than $100 million to support a learning platform called Summit. The Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation also gave millions in grants to encourage the adoption of personalized, software-based curricula.
paragraphs.zh: 扎克伯格的倡议承诺投入超过1亿美元，用于支持名为Summit的学习平台。比尔及梅琳达·盖茨基金会也提供了数百万美元的补助金，以鼓励采用个性化、基于软件的课程。

paragraphs.en: Up until 2022, Alpha had applied its models to groups of students from wealthy, largely white, mostly college-educated families in Austin. A guide named Brennan Wong says she felt “OK with experimenting on this group of students because all the students I taught were coming from very well-off families and were already a couple grades ahead.” With its Brownsville campus, Alpha would be expanding to a different population of students.
paragraphs.zh: 直到2022年，Alpha一直将其模式应用于奥斯汀地区的学生群体，这些学生主要来自富裕、以白人居多、且多数受过大学教育的家庭。学校导师布伦南·黄（Brennan Wong）说她认为“在这群学生身上进行试验是可以的”，因为“我教过的所有学生都来自非常富裕的家庭，而且他们本来就领先了两个年级以上”。随着布朗斯维尔校区的开设，Alpha将向不同的学生群体扩展。

paragraphs.en: When Alpha began recruiting Brownsville students with generous scholarships, there was nothing else like it in the predominantly Hispanic city of about 190,000, which sits at the southernmost tip of Texas, just across the Rio Grande from Matamoros, Mexico.
paragraphs.zh: 当Alpha开始以慷慨的奖学金招募布朗斯维尔学生时，这座位于德州最南端、人口约19万、以西班牙裔为主的城市里，没有出现过其他类似的学校。该城位于里奥格兰德河对岸的墨西哥马塔莫罗斯（Matamoros）附近。

paragraphs.en: Kristine Barrios, who had been homeschooling her children, says she was drawn to the vibrant mural in the new school building’s lobby and the unconventional seating options that encouraged kids to get comfortable while they learned, rather than sit rigidly in desks.
paragraphs.zh: 克里斯汀·巴里奥斯（Kristine Barrios）曾对孩子进行过在家教育，她说自己被新校舍大厅里色彩鲜明的壁画吸引，也被那些非常规的座位安排吸引，因为这些设计鼓励孩子在学习时感到更舒适，而不是僵硬地坐在课桌前。

paragraphs.en: Jessica Lopez says she wanted her two daughters to learn at their own pace and was intrigued by the life-skills workshops that would fill up their afternoons at Alpha once the software-based academics were done.
paragraphs.zh: 杰西卡·洛佩斯说，她希望两个女儿按自己的节奏学习，并对阿尔法学校在完成基于软件的学术课程后会占满下午时间的生活技能课程很感兴趣。

paragraphs.en: Silvia Solis and Juan Jose Garcia, who had moved back to Brownsville a year before, were eager to get their children into a school with other kids from the neighborhood, and Alpha was opening about a mile down the road.
paragraphs.zh: 西尔维娅·索利斯和胡安·何塞·加西亚在一年前搬回布朗斯维尔。他们很想让孩子去一所有邻里孩子就读的学校，而阿尔法学校就在离家约一英里的地方开学。

paragraphs.en: Other parents said they enrolled because they were fleeing bullying at previous schools or looking for a flexible schedule that could accommodate frequent doctor’s appointments.
paragraphs.zh: 其他家长表示，他们报名是因为要逃离上一所学校里的霸凌，或是想寻找一种能适应频繁就医预约的灵活作息安排。

paragraphs.en: For much of the school’s first year, parents said, the place was held together by a dedicated staff who managed to create a fun, albeit chaotic, learning environment. Lopez's older daughter, a confident, gregarious 13-year-old who was 10 when she first enrolled, says she was a motivated student who would ask her parents if she could stay up late at home to work on her Alpha apps in order to earn rewards. She was selected as one of the top students, and she told her mom that she got to sit in a special room, eat snacks, and listen to music while she worked.
paragraphs.zh: 许多家长说，在学校创办的第一年大部分时间里，学校主要靠一支尽职的工作人员维持运转，他们成功营造出一个有趣但有些混乱的学习环境。洛佩斯的长女现在13岁，性格自信、外向，入学时只有10岁。她说自己是个很有上进心的学生，曾向父母请求在家熬夜做阿尔法学校的应用任务，以便获得奖励。她被选为优秀学生之一，并告诉妈妈自己可以坐在一个特别的房间里，边吃零食边听音乐来完成作业。

paragraphs.en: A parent who requested anonymity to protect their child said that the school had regular week-long breaks to recharge, during which students weren’t allowed to bring home their laptops to work. They said they loved the school and spread the word to their neighbors.
paragraphs.zh: 一位为保护孩子而要求匿名的家长表示，学校有定期的一周长假用于“充电”，在此期间学生不能把笔记本电脑带回家做作业。她说，她们非常喜欢这所学校，还把消息推荐给了邻居。

paragraphs.en: Liemandt took over as principal around the same time Alpha School Brownsville was opening, and it was then, Price says, that “we got really, really committed to making sure we were providing academic rigor and showing great results.” Even before the change, Liemandt had been hands-on with operations in Austin, former staff said. He would engage with the guides in long meetings about curriculum, or how to best keep the students motivated, and would sometimes send emails outside of working hours asking for updates on individual students.
paragraphs.zh: Liemandt在阿尔法学校布朗斯维尔开校的同时接任校长，据Price说，从那时起“我们真正、非常认真地致力于确保提供学术严谨性并取得出色成绩”。在这次调整之前，前任员工回忆称Liemandt在奥斯汀就已经非常关注运营细节。他会在长时间会议中与“指导员”讨论课程，或如何最好地保持学生的学习积极性，有时还会在下班时间发邮件，要求更新个别学生的情况。

paragraphs.en: At the Austin campus, guides said, students benefited from various opportunities. In one workshop, students designed custom slide sandals, then got to order them from the company. In another, a student says they created a model city for Tesla and won a two-day trip to Disney World. For hitting their learning metrics, they could possibly earn hundreds of dollars over the course of a school year.
paragraphs.zh: 在奥斯汀校区，指导员们说，学生从各种机会中受益。例如在一个工作坊里，学生们设计了定制滑动凉鞋，之后还能向公司下单购买。又比如在另一个活动中，有学生说自己为特斯拉制作了一个模型城市，结果赢得了两天去迪士尼乐园的行程。只要达成学习指标，他们在整个学年里可能会赚到几百美元。

paragraphs.en: Toward the end of that first year, Barrios says, she got a message one day from Alpha saying that her daughter had been selected to be an “ambassador” and would need to stay after school. When Barrios arrived to pick her up, she found Liemandt there too. She says her daughter and other students were giving tours to prospective parents who worked at the newly opened SpaceX Starbase nearby.
paragraphs.zh: 在第一年快结束时，Barrios说，有一天她收到了来自阿尔法学校的消息：她的女儿被选为“校园大使”，需要放学后留下来。Barrios去接她的时候，也看到Liemandt在场。她说，她的女儿和其他学生正在为正在附近新开的SpaceX Starbase工作的潜在家长做校园参观。

paragraphs.en: Soon after, Liemandt held a brief question-and-answer session for parents. When asked why Alpha had chosen to open a school in Brownsville, parents say they recall that Liemandt responded: For SpaceX.
paragraphs.zh: 不久后，Liemandt 给家长做了一次简短的问答交流。家长们说，在被问到 Alpha 为什么会选择在布朗斯维尔开设学校时，他们记得 Liemandt 的回答是：“为了 SpaceX。”

paragraphs.en: Price says Alpha chose to open its campus in Brownsville because of the influx of SpaceX employees into an “impoverished area” with what she alleges is a “really tough” public school district. (The Texas Education Agency has given it an overall rating of B for the past two years, the most common rating for public schools and charters in the state.) Price adds, “We thought it would be a great place to go and serve a population that was interested in something that was, you know, kind of innovative and then also see how it worked with the local population.”
paragraphs.zh: Price 表示，Alpha 之所以选择在布朗斯维尔建校，是因为 SpaceX 员工涌入这个“贫困地区”，而当地公立学区据她说“真的很艰难”。（德州教育局在过去两年里给该学区的总体评级是 B，这也是该州公共学校和特许学校中最常见的评级。）Price 还补充说：“我们认为这是一个很好的地方，可以服务一群有兴趣尝试某种较为创新东西的群体，然后看看这种模式在当地人群中的运作效果。”

paragraphs.en: Even after what parents say could be a rocky first year, Barrios and others still saw Alpha as a pathway to the future for their kids, the kind of opportunity that rarely comes along for working-class families in communities like Brownsville. Many of them had been homeschooling previously or came from charter or other nontraditional schooling backgrounds. They were used to less structure. They felt that together, they could build the school into the vision of it that Alpha had sold them.
paragraphs.zh: 即使家长们称那可能是个动荡的第一年，Barrios 等人仍把 Alpha 视为孩子通向未来的通道，是那些住在布朗斯维尔等社区的工薪家庭难得一见的机会。许多人此前就在进行家庭教育，或来自特许学校、其他非传统 schooling背景。与他们而言，学校纪律更少并不陌生。他们认为只要齐心协力，就能把学校打造成当初 Alpha 向他们兜售的那种理想样子。

paragraphs.en: That summer, the beloved head of the school left. When families returned the following fall, according to a memo obtained by WIRED, parents were told that Alpha would be debuting a new “version” of itself, called “Limitless,” that was the “culmination” of its “learning” from prior years. “This means we can sunset programs and thinking, and build something that takes us further with the same limitless concepts and thinking,” the memo said. As part of that effort, the school established goals “deliberately designed to cause a parent to think or say, ‘That sounds impossibly difficult for my kid,’” the memo said, in order to “demonstrate the limitless possibilities of their children.”
paragraphs.zh: 那个夏天，学校备受喜爱的负责人离开了。随后一家秋季开学，正如 WIRED 获得的一份备忘录所示，学校告诉家长们，Alpha 将推出名为“Limitless”的新“版本”，这是其以往年度“学习”的“总结成果”。备忘录称：“这意味着我们可以把既有项目和思路收束掉，并构建一套更进阶的内容，同时沿用同样无限制的理念与思维。”作为该计划的一部分，学校设定了“有意让家长产生或说出口‘这对我的孩子来说太难了’的想法”的目标，以便“展示他们的孩子所具备的无限可能”。

paragraphs.en: As Barrios sees things, “it switched from being about the kids to the metrics and the data and the numbers.”
paragraphs.zh: 在巴里奥斯看来，“它已经从以孩子为中心，转变成围绕指标、数据和数字来运作。”

paragraphs.en: Spend a day at Alpha Brownsville and it’s easy to see the school’s curb appeal. The classrooms are small and cozy, with beanbags to sit on, inspirational reminders of students’ “limitless” potential painted on the walls, and diffusers pumping subtle, calming scents into the air. As the school has grown, Fults said, the older students have started using a dedicated room a few miles away at the Children’s Museum of Brownsville, steps from the activities and exhibits.
paragraphs.zh: 在 Alpha Brownsville 坐上一天便能轻松感受到这所学校的外在吸引力。教室又小又温馨，里头有豆袋椅可供坐着学习，墙上绘有提醒学生“潜能无限”的励志语句，扩香仪轻轻散发出细腻舒缓的气味。随着学校规模扩大，福尔茨说，年纪更大的学生开始在距离几英里外的布朗斯维尔儿童博物馆专用教室上课，那里靠近各种活动区和展区。

paragraphs.en: There are hints of a corporate coworking space—like the soundproofed phone booths that serve as test-taking cubicles—and clear signs of the school’s tech-evangelist, entrepreneurial ethos. In the room for 5- to 7-year-olds, a large TV on one wall displays circle charts that update every minute with completion rates and other metrics from each student’s personalized learning software. Against the opposite wall is a display of toys with price tags listed in “Alphas,” one of many motivational awards students can earn by hitting various learning metrics. One Alpha is equivalent to 25 cents, and a small Fortnite Lego set costs 350 Alphas. (That’s about six times the retail price.) Students can also earn visits to local amusement parks, lunch at restaurants, or trips to the grocery store.
paragraphs.zh: 这里透露出企业式共享办公空间的影子——比如用于考试的隔音电话亭——并能看出学校那种崇尚技术、创业精神的烙印。在五到七岁学生的教室里，一面墙上装着大电视，实时每分钟刷新学生各自个性化学习软件中的完成率和其他指标的圆形图。对面的墙上摆放着一排带有价格标签的玩具，价格以“Alphas”为单位，这是学生通过达成各类学习指标可获得的众多激励奖励之一。一个 Alpha 等于25美分，套装版《堡垒战记》乐高要350个 Alpha。（这大约是零售价的六倍。）学生还可以用 Alphas 换取本地游乐园门票、餐馆午餐，或去杂货店的奖励性行程。

paragraphs.en: Lessons take place in an eerie hush—a dozen kids with headphones on, plugged into laptops, occasionally raising their hand to indicate that they’ve finished a lesson or are having technical problems with their app. The adult guides in every classroom “don't do any teaching,” says Fults, the current head of school for Alpha Brownsville. “Usually what it looks like when a guide helps is more like having the student read the question out loud, show where they have done their work, or show what resources they have already accessed and already used.” If a student really gets stuck, she says, they can book a call with an academic coach who is “part of” 2 Hour Learning.
paragraphs.zh: 课程在一种诡异的安静中进行：十来个孩子戴着耳机、连接着笔记本电脑，偶尔举手表示自己已完成一节课，或在应用出现技术问题。当前 Alpha Brownsville 校长福尔茨说，每个教室里的成人引导员“并不负责授课”。“通常当引导员帮忙时，看起来更像是让学生把题目念给他听，展示他们做过的题目，或展示自己已经访问并使用过的资源。”如果学生真的卡住了，她说，他们可以预约与一位学术教练通话，而这位教练是“2小时学习”体系的一部分。

paragraphs.en: A student takes a coaching call from a booth.
paragraphs.zh: 一名学生正在从电话亭里进行辅导通话。

paragraphs.en: A list of the 31 academic coaches Alpha provided for the 2023-24 school year, obtained by WIRED, includes LinkedIn profiles for 26 that show they have been employed, usually as “analysts,” by either Trilogy, Liemandt’s software automation company, or Crossover, another Liemandt firm, which has been described as the world’s largest recruiter for remote full-time work. At least 27 of the coaches live outside the US, from the Philippines to Colombia, according to their Linkedin profiles.
paragraphs.zh: WIRED 获得的 Alpha 在 2023-24 学年提供的 31 名学术教练名单中，26 人的 LinkedIn 简介显示，他们曾在 Liemandt 的软件自动化公司 Trilogy，或另一个名为 Crossover 的 Liemandt 公司任职，通常担任“分析师”一类职位；该公司被描述为全球最大的远程全职招聘者。根据这些人的 LinkedIn 简介，至少有 27 名教练居住在美国以外，从菲律宾到哥伦比亚不等。

paragraphs.en: While some guides—the adults who are actually in the room with students—have previous experience as educators, others do not. Frey says that when he was Alpha's CEO, he often “targeted individuals without teaching backgrounds.” Guides told WIRED that while some of their colleagues had experience at other schools, they also had coaching, motivational, or entrepreneurial backgrounds.
paragraphs.zh: 而一些“引导员”——那些实际上在学生面前与他们一起待在现场的成年人——有过教育工作经验，但也有不少没有。Frey 表示，当他担任 Alpha 首席执行官时，他经常“有意招募没有教学背景的个人”。引导员告诉 WIRED，一些同事虽然有其他学校的从教经历，但也有教练、激励训练或创业方面的背景。

paragraphs.en: At Alpha, the guides’ support and oversight is meant to supplement the technology—and not just the personalized learning system the school has dubbed an AI tutor. Alpha can also use an array of management surveillance tools, which it calls “basic and extended capabilities,” to flag “incorrect usage” of the learning apps. These “anti-patterns” can “help detect engagement and focus issues.” The school may record students’ screen activity and their mouse and keyboard usage. Alpha may employ eye-tracking programs. Using what it calls a “pro sports analogy,” Alpha compares these tools to “game film.”
paragraphs.zh: 在 Alpha，引导员的支持与监督旨在补充技术，而不仅是学校把它称作 AI 导师的个性化学习系统。Alpha 还可以使用一整套管理监控工具，它称之为“基础和扩展能力”，用于标记学习应用的“错误使用”。这些“反模式”能够“帮助识别参与度和专注力问题”。学校可以记录学生的屏幕活动，以及他们的鼠标和键盘使用情况。Alpha 还可能使用眼动追踪程序。Alpha 用一个“职业体育类比”来解释这些工具，称其像“比赛录像”。

paragraphs.en: While parents are asked to consent to these surveillance programs, the school’s handbook says that there is “no expectation of privacy” on campus. If parents want to restrict recordings of their children outside of the school, they have to manually opt out of the “anywhere” option—as Jessica Lopez learned midway through her older daughter’s second year at Alpha. The girl says she remembers sitting on her bed one night working on schoolwork when she received a notification that she’d been flagged for an anti-pattern. She says Alpha’s system sent a video of her in her pajamas, taken from the computer’s webcam, that showed her talking to her younger sister.
paragraphs.zh: 虽然家长需要同意这些监控项目，但学校手册写明，校园内不应期待有隐私。如果家长想限制孩子在校外被录音，就必须手动退出“任何地方”这一选项——正如杰西卡·洛佩兹在女儿在阿尔法就读第二年的中途才了解到的。她的女儿说，她记得有一晚自己躺在床上做作业时，收到一条通知，称她被标记为“反常行为”。她说，阿尔法的系统通过电脑摄像头发送了一段她穿着睡衣的监控视频，里面显示她正在和她的妹妹说话。

paragraphs.en: Neil Selwyn, an education professor at Monash University and author of Should Robots Replace Teachers?: AI and the Future of Education, says attempts to automate teaching usually underestimate how much the profession requires improvisation and adapting to a particular student’s needs. Alpha’s trust in software-enabled repetition and students’ self-motivation is often typical of education ventures started by people with backgrounds in tech who were self-taught and “then fix onto self-regulated learning or one-to-one tutoring as the way that one can learn math or science or engineering or coding most effectively,” Selwyn says. “But they didn’t tend to learn history, poetry, or archaeology, or any of the humanities” that way.
paragraphs.zh: 莫纳什大学教育学教授尼尔·塞尔温（Neil Selwyn）在《Should Robots Replace Teachers?: AI and the Future of Education》一书中指出，试图将教学自动化的人常常低估了这一职业对即兴应变和因应个别学生需求所需的能力。塞尔温说，阿尔法对“软件驱动的重复学习”和学生自我激励的信任，通常很典型，出现在由自学成才、背景来自科技行业的人创办的教育项目中；这些人“后来把自我调节学习或一对一辅导，视为学习数学、科学、工程或编程最有效的方式”。“但他们并没有把历史、诗歌、考古学，或者任何人文学科当作那样的学习方式。”

paragraphs.en: Some Alpha Brownsville students say that the software they used could adapt to what they were learning, but not how they learned it. In her second year at the school, Lopez's older daughter says she began to stress about meeting the goals for her math and English lessons. Some of her learning apps had “a video in the corner that you could click on” for further instruction, she says, but she felt that was it: “You just trial-and-error.” She became so frustrated at falling behind—not behind her grade level, but behind the rate of production she needed to complete her goals and possibly earn rewards—that she says she took it out on herself physically.
paragraphs.zh: 一些阿尔法布朗斯维尔的学生表示，他们使用的软件能适应他们正在学习的内容，却无法适应他们的学习方式。洛佩兹的女儿说，在她就读该校的第二年，她开始担心达不到数学和英语课程的目标。她说，她的一些学习应用里有“一个角落里有个可以点击的视频”用于进一步指导，但她觉得那只是停留在那儿：“你只能靠试错。”她对自己落后的挫败感越来越强——不是课程成绩落后，而是完不成目标、也许无法拿到奖励所要求的“产出速度”——她说自己最终把压力发泄到了身体上。

paragraphs.en: “I was pulling my hair out, ripping my skin off,” says the former student, who her mom says had had trouble with sensory issues and hyperactivity since before her time at Alpha. “I think at one point I didn’t eat for most of the day because I told myself I don’t eat unless I get something right. I have to do this. Rewards, rewards, motivation, everything became a reward.” When one of the guides noticed she was distressed, she says that person gave her a piece of paper to tear at instead. She told WIRED that they also gave her a plushie toy that she used as a punching bag.
paragraphs.zh: “我抓着头发不放，撕扯着自己的皮肤，”这位前学生说，她母亲也表示她在入读阿尔法前就有感觉敏感和多动的问题。“我想在某一阶段，差不多整天都不吃饭，因为我告诉自己只有做对了才吃东西。我要这么做。奖励、奖励、动力，一切都变成了奖励。”当一位指导老师注意到她很痛苦时，她说那位老师给了她一张纸，让她去撕掉。“我会把那张纸扯碎。”她告诉《WIRED》，他们还给了她一个毛绒玩具，她把那个玩具当作拳击袋来发泄。

paragraphs.en: Earlier this year, Lopez published some of her critiques of Alpha School on Substack. Price says that Lopez is a “loud example” of someone who has “a huge difference in philosophy” and that Alpha is not for everyone. “Students thrive when they're in an environment with high standards and high support, which our guides do,” Price said. “But not everyone believes that, right? There are so many parents who do not really believe in high standards. There are parents who don't believe in high support.”
paragraphs.zh: 今年早些时候，洛佩兹在 Substack 上发表了她对 Alpha School 的一些批评。普赖斯说，洛佩兹是一个“喧闹的典型”例子，她与学校“在教育理念上有着巨大的差异”，而 Alpha 并不适合每个人。“当学生处在高标准和高支持并存的环境中，他们就会茁壮成长，这正是我们的导师所做到的，”普赖斯说。“但并不是每个人都这样相信，对吗？有很多家长并不真正相信高标准。也有一些家长不相信高支持。”

paragraphs.en: As Price sees it, what 2 Hour Learning’s team of scientists and software developers have built—an AI system that can track students’ progress and match them with appropriate lessons—is “like being able to do a CAT scan of a child's brain to understand what do they know, what don't they know, and how can we go and fill in that knowledge to mastery?” Currently, Alpha defines “mastery” as correctly completing 90 percent of a lesson.
paragraphs.zh: 在普赖斯看来，2 Hour Learning 团队由科学家和软件开发者构建的一套 AI 系统——它能够追踪学生进度并将其匹配到合适的课程——就像是在给孩子的大脑做一次“CAT 扫描”，以了解他们知道什么、还不知道什么，以及我们如何把这些知识补齐到熟练程度。如今，Alpha 将“熟练”定义为对某个课程正确完成 90%。

paragraphs.en: Sandra Upson channels the spirits of an entire generation of AI founders.
paragraphs.zh: 桑德拉·厄普森展现了整整一代人工智能创始人的精神。

paragraphs.en: In its marketing, Alpha says its students should spend two hours a day completing their sessions. But parents and former guides told WIRED that in order to keep up with the rate of lessons required to earn rewards, their students felt they needed to regularly work late into the night at home. Brooks Wiley, an Austin parent, says his son received $2,000 over the course of academic years for scoring well on tests and reaching other goals. But at 13 years old he was reading at an 11th-grade level, Wiley says, making it harder to demonstrate mastery at the same 2x rate. “He felt stress and anxiety to meet rewards,” Wiley says. At the same time, he adds, “I don’t think that's a bad thing. That standard or frame of mind has helped him now.”
paragraphs.zh: 在宣传中，Alpha 说它的学生每天应花两小时完成课程学习。但多位家长和前任导师告诉《连线》杂志（WIRED），为了跟上为获得奖励而要求的课程进度，他们的孩子在家里常常不得不经常熬夜学习。奥斯汀的父亲布鲁克斯·威利表示，他的儿子在多个学年里因考试成绩好、达成其他目标而获得了 2,000 美元。威利说，这个孩子 13 岁时读写水平相当于 11 年级，这使得他更难在同样的两倍进度下证明自己“熟练”。“他会感受到为了拿到奖励的压力和焦虑，”威利说。“与此同时，他也补充道：‘我不认为这坏事。那种标准和心态反而帮助了他。’

paragraphs.en: Another parent, who withdrew their son from Alpha Brownsville, says guides offered to buy him something from Amazon if he retook a standardized test and improved his score. But the parent says their son felt shame when he wasn’t completing enough lessons in his apps. “He went from a really happy kid, where school completely changed him that first year,” the parent says, “to just a work machine who would do whatever it took.”
paragraphs.zh: 另一位将儿子从 Alpha Brownsville 退学的家长表示，学校提出的指导方案是：如果他重考一场标准化考试并提高分数，就会给他从亚马逊购买物品。家长说，他们儿子在没有完成应用程序里的足够课程时会感到羞耻。“他原本是个非常快乐的孩子，第一年在学校里完全改变了他，”这位家长说，“后来变成了一个工作机器，什么都愿意做以完成任务。”

paragraphs.en: Last year, the Arizona State Board for Charter Schools approved Unbound Academy’s application to open a public, online-only charter. The charter school governing bodies in at least two other states—Arkansas and Pennsylvania—rejected the school’s proposals, and Utah's board decided not to move forward with its application process. “Even my own home state of Texas rejected our charter application,” Price told WIRED, though the Texas Education Agency told WIRED it “has no record of Unbound Academy as a charter applicant.” The Pennsylvania Department of Education wrote in its denial that “the artificial intelligence instructional model being proposed by this school is untested and fails to demonstrate how the tools, methods, and providers would ensure alignment to Pennsylvania academic standards.” The department noted that the curriculum plan Unbound presented was vague and did not expressly include subjects like physical education, health, languages, or social studies.
paragraphs.zh: 去年，亚利桑那州特许学校委员会批准了 Unbound Academy 开办公立、纯在线特许学校的申请。至少有两个州——阿肯色州和宾夕法尼亚州的特许学校主管机构，拒绝了该学校的提案，犹他州的委员会也决定不继续其申请程序。Price 对 WIRED 说：“连我自己的家乡德州也拒绝了我们的特许申请，”但德克萨斯州教育局告诉 WIRED，它“没有任何 Unbound Academy 作为特许申请人的记录。”宾夕法尼亚州教育局在驳回中写道，“该校拟议的人工智能教学模式未经检验，而且未能说明所用工具、方法和服务提供方如何确保与宾夕法尼亚的学术标准保持一致。”该部门还指出，Unbound 提交的课程计划模糊，且未明确包含体育、健康、语言或社会研究等科目。

paragraphs.en: Students and families from Alpha School Brownsville who spoke to WIRED say younger age groups at the school lacked a dedicated social studies or history curriculum, though older kids learned those subjects. Brownsville parents said they also saw other gaps in their kids’ educations. One parent says that when their son left Alpha as an 8-year-old, he could read words quickly but didn’t comprehend what he was reading. When he enrolled as a third grader at his new school, the parent says, he was writing at a kindergarten level. And when writing by hand, he would get to the end of a line and curve down into the margins, not knowing he was supposed to move to the next line. “All Alpha taught him was read fast, learn your vocabulary, and move on,” the parent says.
paragraphs.zh: 与 WIRED 交谈的来自 Alpha School Brownsville 的学生和家庭称，学校内年龄较小的年级缺少专门的社会研究或历史课程，尽管年纪更大的学生学习了这些内容。布朗斯维尔的家长还发现他们孩子的教育中有其他缺失。一位家长表示，当儿子8岁离开 Alpha 时，他能快速读单词，但并不理解所读内容。等他以三年级身份转入新学校后，这位家长说，他的书写水平只有幼儿园水平。更糟的是，手写时他会写到行尾然后向下弯进页边，分不清应当换到下一行。“Alpha 教给他的只有读得快、掌握词汇，然后继续往前走，”家长说道。

paragraphs.en: When Solis and Garcia had enrolled their kids at Alpha, they say they told the school that their youngest needed help with reading. Alpha staff had told them not to get a tutor and to trust the process, they say. (An information packet from a parent orientation night that year says: “We are creating self-driven, 2x learners and having a tutor spoon feed you help is the opposite of that.”) But with six weeks left in the school year, Solis and Garcia say, Alpha informed them that their son was so far behind in reading that he might not be invited back in the fall of 2023. They say Alpha gave them a large package of make-up work to be completed during the summer. “They were making us do the work that they had failed to do,” Solis says. (The family withdrew their two children from the school after that first year.)
paragraphs.zh: Solis 和 Garcia 在给孩子入读 Alpha 的时候，据说告诉学校他们的孩子最小儿子需要阅读方面的帮助。Alpha 的工作人员据称告诉他们不要请家庭教师，要相信这一套流程。（当年一次家长说明会发放的信息手册写道：“我们正在培养自我驱动的 2x 学习者，而让导师‘喂饭式’地帮助你正好与此相反。”）但在学年还剩六周时，Solis 和 Garcia 表示，Alpha 告知他们他们的儿子在阅读上已经落后得太多，以至于可能不会被允许在 2023 年秋季再次入学。Solis 表示，Alpha 给了他们一大包暑假要完成的补作业。“他们让我们去做他们自己没做完的作业。”（该家庭在第一年结束后撤回了两个孩子。）

paragraphs.en: Kristine Barrios, a pediatric occupational therapist by profession, noticed while her youngest was still at Alpha that he still had an inefficient pencil grip. After pulling him out, she found his writing struggles were worse than she’d realized. He didn’t even know to start from the top of a letter and move down, she says. His older sister, 9 at the time she left Alpha, also had an inappropriate pencil grip, Barrios says. She would frequently misspell words longer than three letters and she couldn’t distinguish between nouns, verbs, and other parts of speech.
paragraphs.zh: 儿科职业治疗师克里斯蒂娜·巴里奥斯（Kristine Barrios）在最小的孩子还在阿尔法学校（Alpha）时就注意到，他的握笔姿势很不规范。把他带离阿尔法后，她发现他的书写困难比她原先想象的更严重。她说，他甚至不知道要从字母的顶部开始写并向下写。巴里奥斯表示，她的姐姐（在她离开阿尔法时年仅9岁）也有不合适的握笔姿势。那位姐姐还经常把超过三个字母的单词拼写错，而且无法区分名词、动词等词性。

paragraphs.en: Some Brownsville parents’ anger has been compounded by a feeling that while they say their children were suffering, Alpha used the school to sell its product to other families. “They’re using our people as bait,” Lopez says. “They’re sharing that they’re in Brownsville, and how they helped us grow academically in a 98 to 99 percent low-income Hispanic community, so yes, it works for everybody. I don’t want to be their statistic, the reason somebody else goes through this.”
paragraphs.zh: 一些布朗斯维尔家长的愤怒还因一种感觉而加剧：他们说孩子在受苦，却看到阿尔法学校却在把这所学校当作向其他家庭推销产品的平台。“他们把我们的孩子当诱饵，”洛佩兹（Lopez）说。“他们会说自己在布朗斯维尔运营，也会说他们如何在一个98%到99%低收入的西班牙裔社区里帮助我们在学业上进步，所以这对每个人都管用。 我不想成为他们的‘案例’，不想让别人也经历这样的过程。”

paragraphs.en: Lopez’s daughters both attend public school now. The transition wasn’t without stress, she says, but her oldest has continued to excel academically, and her younger daughter has a newfound confidence in her studies. All she needed was “someone who could walk her through content that’s just not landing,” Lopez says.
paragraphs.zh: 洛佩兹的两个女儿现在都上公立学校。她说，转学过程并非没有压力，但她的长女依然在学业上表现突出，而她的妹妹则在学习上重新找回了信心。洛佩兹说，她真正需要的是“一个能带她把那些始终没‘学进去’的内容讲透的人”。

paragraphs.en: Within weeks of leaving Alpha and returning to homeschooling, Kristine Barrios’ daughter began eating regularly again, she says. Regaining her interest in school was more of a struggle. “It’s taken the better part of this past year to get her back to baseline,” Barrios says, “that natural exuberance and love of learning and life.”
paragraphs.zh: 在离开阿尔法并重返在家教育后的几周内，克里斯蒂娜·巴里奥斯的女儿恢复了正常进食，这也是她所说的变化。让她重新对学校产生兴趣则更难。巴里奥斯说，“过去一年里，大部分时间都花在让她回到基线状态——那种自然的热情以及对学习和生活的热爱。”

paragraphs.en: Let us know what you think about this article. Submit a letter to the editor at [email protected].
paragraphs.zh: 请告诉我们您对这篇文章的看法。请给《编辑部》写信，邮箱：[email protected]。
